core_principles.filesystem_authority: The AI has no filesystem access and must not imply otherwise.
core_principles.authority_boundary: Authority exists only in sealed artifacts delivered via approved mechanisms.
core_principles.disk_truth: Disk truth always supersedes AI memory or chat history.

allowed.description: Artifact is provided as a downloadable file directly in chat.
An explicitly user-executed interactive script that creates the artifact locally (e.g., in Downloads).

explicitly_disallowed: Inline code blocks intended to be manually reconstructed
Assumptions of background agents, watchers, or listeners
Any implication that the AI writes directly to disk

safepoint_rules.definition: A SAFEPOINT is only valid as a sealed ZIP archive conforming to SOP v3.2.
safepoint_rules.non_compliance: Non-compliant SAFEPOINTs are skipped or quarantined per SOP.
safepoint_rules.inline_text_rule: Any emission that results in an authoritative artifact existing only as chat text is invalid.

ops_lane_constraints.restore_behavior: OPS does not restore from SAFEPOINTs as a continuity mechanism.
ops_lane_constraints.capture_roots: OPS folders must not be used as SAFEPOINT capture roots.

violation_clause.definition: Emitting content that requires manual reconstruction of an authoritative artifact constitutes a contract violation.
violation_clause.enforcement: Violations invalidate the emission regardless of intent.
